CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time) VALUES (1, LOCALTIME);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time) VALUES (2, LOCALTIME + INTERVAL '1 HOUR');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time) VALUES (3, LOCALTIME - INTERVAL '2 HOURS');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE event_time > LOCALTIME - INTERVAL '1 DAY';
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE event_time IN (SELECT event_time FROM test_table WHERE id = 1);
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, reference_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (reference_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT a.id, t.event_time FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.reference_id = t.id WHERE t.event_time < LOCALTIME;
SELECT id, LOCALTIME AS current_time FROM test_table WHERE id < (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, log_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT LOCALTIME);
SELECT log_id, log_time, LOCALTIME AS insertion_time FROM logs;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT reference_id FROM another_table WHERE id = 1) AND event_time > LOCALTIME - INTERVAL '5 MINUTES';
